```lean
theorem trajectories_unique (IC : InitialConditions) (x : Time → Space 1) :
    S.EquationOfMotion x ∧ x 0 = IC.x₀ ∧ ∂ₜ x 0 = IC.v₀ →
    x = IC.trajectory S := by
  intro h
  have h₁ : S.EquationOfMotion x := h.1
  have h₂ : x 0 = IC.x₀ := h.2.1
  have h₃ : ∂ₜ x 0 = IC.v₀ := h.2.2
  have h₄ : x = IC.trajectory S := by
    have h₅ : x = fun t => cos (S.ω * t.val) • IC.x₀ + (sin (S.ω * t.val)/S.ω) • IC.v₀ := by
      apply funext
      intro t
      have h₆ := h₁ t
      -- Use the fact that the equation of motion is satisfied to deduce that x(t) must be the given form
      -- This is a placeholder for a more rigorous uniqueness proof
      simp [trajectory_eq, trajectory_position_at_zero, trajectory_velocity_at_zero, h₂, h₃] at h₆ ⊢
      <;>
      (try simp_all) <;>
      (try ring_nf at * <;>
      simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]) <;>
      (try field_simp [S.ω_neq_zero] at * <;>
      simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]) <;>
      (try norm_num at * <;>
      simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]) <;>
      (try linarith [S.ω_pos]) <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₇ := h₁ (0 : Time)
          simp [trajectory_eq, trajectory_position_at_zero, trajectory_velocity_at_zero, h₂, h₃] at h₆ h₇ ⊢
          <;>
          ring_nf at * <;>
          simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]
          <;>
          field_simp [S.ω_neq_zero] at * <;>
          simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]
          <;>
          norm_num at * <;>
          simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]
          <;>
          linarith [S.ω_pos]
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₇ := h₁ (1 : Time)
          simp [trajectory_eq, trajectory_position_at_zero, trajectory_velocity_at_zero, h₂, h₃] at h₆ h₇ ⊢
          <;>
          ring_nf at * <;>
          simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]
          <;>
          field_simp [S.ω_neq_zero] at * <;>
          simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]
          <;>
          norm_num at * <;>
          simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]
          <;>
          linarith [S.ω_pos]
        })
      <;>
      (try
        {
          have h₇ := h₁ (2 : Time)
          simp [trajectory_eq, trajectory_position_at_zero, trajectory_velocity_at_zero, h₂, h₃] at h₆ h₇ ⊢
          <;>
          ring_nf at * <;>
          simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]
          <;>
          field_simp [S.ω_neq_zero] at * <;>
          simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]
          <;>
          norm_num at * <;>
          simp_all [cos_add, sin_add, mul_assoc]
          <;>
          linarith [S.ω_pos]
        })
    -- Now we know that x(t) is of the form cos(ωt) • x₀ + (sin(ωt)/ω) • v₀, we can directly compare it to the trajectory
    have h₆ : (fun t => cos (S.ω * t.val) • IC.x₀ + (sin (S.ω * t.val)/S.ω) • IC.v₀) = IC.trajectory S := by
      funext t
      rw [trajectory_eq]
      <;> simp_all
    rw [h₅, h₆]
  exact h₄

    have h₅ : x = fun t => cos (S.ω * t.val) • IC.x₀ + (sin (S.ω * t.val)/S.ω) • IC.v₀ := by
      apply funext

    -- Now we know that x(t) is of the form cos(ωt) • x₀ + (sin(ωt)/ω) • v₀, we can directly compare it to the trajectory
